title: Community Relations: Nonschool Use of School Facilities
description: This policy governs how school buildings, grounds, and other facilities can be used by outside groups and organizations when school is not in session. It applies to community members, local organizations, and groups — including religious organizations — who may want to rent or borrow school spaces for meetings, events, or activities. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it sets the rules for who can access school facilities, under what conditions, and on what terms, helping ensure that public school property is used fairly and appropriately by the broader community.

timeline_local.summary: The board is discussing (but not voting on at this meeting) revisions to the local nonschool use of school facilities policy. This is listed as a discussion item only, not an action item.

timeline_local.notes: Listed under 'Board Policy Discussion Item' (Section X.A) as 'Discuss Revisions to Board Policy GKD (Local) Community Relations: Nonschool Use of School Facilities.' No adoption vote indicated at this meeting. Redline detail not included in these pages.

timeline_local.summary: Information regarding proposed changes to Board Policy GKD (Local) and Facility Rental Fees was presented to the Board by the Superintendent and Assistant Superintendent of Operations. No action was taken at the December 16, 2025 meeting.
timeline_local.notes: This was a discussion item only at the December 16, 2025 meeting (Item B under Administration). No specific amendment language was described in the minutes. The meeting packet for the January 20, 2026 meeting (the approval date for these minutes) may contain the actual GKD revisions for action.

timeline_local.summary: A new section titled 'Religious Organization Use' is being added to policy GKD (Local). This section explicitly addresses the conditions under which a religious organization may use district facilities, requiring that such use not interfere with the District's educational mission, that the organization follow standard terms and pay standard fees (unless waived by the Board), and that the organization be liable for any damages incurred during facility use.
changes_added: Religious Organization Use section (entire section is new, displayed in red/underlined): 'The District may allow a religious organization to use the district's or school's facilities to host religious worship, services, sermons, or assemblies only if the facility use does not interfere with the District's educational mission (see GKD Legal for clarification). A religious organization shall follow the District's standard terms and pay the District's standard fees for usage, unless the Board waives the fees. A religious organization shall be liable for any damages while utilizing district facilities.'

### Item B: Facility Rental Fees & Policy GKD (Discussion Only, No Action)

Trustees Horton and Semmler initiated this review. Robert McCord (Assistant Superintendent of Operations) presented background and current status:

**Background:**
- Online rental system went digital in 2012
- In July 2024, the board was informed the district was losing $150,000–$160,000 on facility rentals over an 11-month period; rates were below surrounding dis

The remainder of the policy (Scheduling Priorities, Approval of Use, Release of Liability, Private Lessons/Clinics/Summer Athletic Leagues, and Special Provisions sections) appears to be existing, unchanged text.
